title: /etc/hosts has wrong content
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If container is started with --network=host /etc/hosts maps hostname to wrong IP address (127.0.1.1).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -it --rm --network host registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34 cat /etc/hosts`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman run -it --rm --network host registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34 cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1       localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1             localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
127.0.1.1 foo.mydomain.com foo.mydomain.com optimistic_newton
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
podman run -it --rm --network host registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34 cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1       localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1             localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
192.168.1.72 foo.mydomain.com foo.mydomain.com optimistic_newton
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.16.5
Built:        Fri Jul 16 22:33:32 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Why would you need `192.168.1.72` as entry in /etc/hosts? Is this your eth0 ip address? I do not think podman can figure out the correct ip. You could have several interfaces with several ip adresses. According to the `hosts` man page:
> 127.0.1.1 is often used for the FQDN of the machine

I close this as duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10319.

To be honest, I was sure there is an issue on this topic, but I couldn't find it.

My description what I expect is wrong, I'm not expecting any IP address in `/etc/hosts` file if `--network=host` is passed as argument, also not `127.0.1.1`. Problem with this entry is that a service as consequence starts listening on `127.0.1.1`, and this is wrong. In docker, if you run `hostname -i` in container which is started with `--network=host` you are getting the same IP address like on host machine. Podman returns now `127.0.1.1`, but this was not the case in older version (on RHEL 7).

The problem came to light when a developer in our company complained that he could no longer connect to a service from a remote machine (after upgrade to RHEL 8). `netstat -tulpen` showed where the problem is: `127.0.1.1:7105`, so you can access the service from local machine, but not from remote.
